onEnter: const hasWebRoot = SC.kernel.state.nodes.some(n => n.type === 'web-root'); if(!hasWebRoot) { const n = SC.kernel.addNode({ title: 'New Web App', type: 'web-root' }); SC.kernel.selectNode(n.id); SC.kernel.bridge.fetchTemplateData('tpl_web_standard').then(data => { SC.kernel.applyTemplateToNode(n.id, data); SC.render(); }); } else { const p = SC.kernel.state.nodes.find(n => n.type === 'web-root'); SC.kernel.selectNode(p.id); SC.render(); }
text: The Web Phase allows you to construct User Interfaces natively. I've imported a web template for us. Let's switch to the Web Phase to view it.
The central viewport transforms into a visual HTML renderer and drag-and-drop workspace.
To edit the raw HTML, open the Inspector.
You can add raw CSS directly to nodes, and it will be rendered live in the view.
Finally, let's return to the Map Phase.
